project_overview.project_name: One WASH—Consolidated Water Supply, Sanitation, and Hygiene Account Project (One WASH—CWA)
project_overview.project_development_objective: To increase access to safe water supply, sanitation, and hygiene services and strengthen capacity for water resources management and service delivery.
project_overview.total_financing: US$350 million (IDA Credit: US$300 million, Counterpart Funding: US$50 million)
project_overview.beneficiaries: Approximately 3 million people (2 million rural, 1 million urban), 2,800 health facilities and schools, 8,260 WASHCOMs, 180 utilities

geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: partial - project includes areas affected by sporadic unrest, displacement, and climate shocks; Ethiopia hosts 920,000+ refugees and has internally displaced populations

key_components.component_name: Rural Water Supply, Sanitation, and Hygiene
key_components.description: Increase access to safe WSS services in selected rural woredas, enhance service delivery management capacity

key_components.component_name: Urban Water Supply, Sanitation, and Hygiene
key_components.description: Improve access to WSS services in small- and medium-sized towns, strengthen operational efficiency of utilities

key_components.component_name: Institutional Water Supply, Sanitation, and Hygiene
key_components.description: Improve integrated access to advanced WASH facilities in schools and health centers

key_components.component_name: Climate Resilient Water Supply, Sanitation, and Hygiene
key_components.description: Strengthen water resources management, increase access in flood/drought-prone areas, contingency emergency response
Operationalize sector MIS, institutional strengthening and project management support

implementation_arrangements.implementing_agency: Ministry of Water, Irrigation and Energy (MoWIE) through Water Development Commission (WDC), Ministry of Education (MoE), Ministry of Health (MoH), Ministry of Finance (MoF)
implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: National WASH Steering Committee (NWSC) chaired by MoWIE Minister, National WASH Coordination Office (NWCO), Regional WASH Steering Committees, Woreda WASH Teams
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: World Bank Procurement Regulations for IPF, National Open Competitive Procurement (NOCP), decentralized implementation through regional bureaus
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: Quarterly reporting through WASH MIS, National WASH Inventory, community-based monitoring tools, annual review meetings

identified_risks.risk_description: Political and social unrest creating difficulties in mobility and access to communities
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Strengthen coordination between federal and regional governments, community sensitization, annual planning to shift focus to feasible areas

identified_risks.risk_description: Increased complexity in addressing water resources management and climate adaptation challenges
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Technical assistance, readiness criteria, stepped approach for institutional capacity building

identified_risks.risk_description: Limited capacity of WASHCOMs, boards, and utilities to plan and manage infrastructure sustainably
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Increased tailored capacity building, functional MIS for tracking, local and regional government response mechanisms

identified_risks.risk_description: Decentralized implementation, staffing capacity and turnover, weak FM and procurement capacity
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Detailed capacity building, strengthening implementing agencies, improved internal controls and audit arrangements

identified_risks.risk_description: High staff turnover, capacity gaps at regional/woreda levels, GBV risks from water collection and poor sanitation
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Continued training, expanded CFT terms of reference, strengthened GRM, zero-tolerance GBV policy

identified_risks.risk_description: Water resource conflicts, displacement pressures, conflicting priorities among implementing agencies
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Enhanced environmental and social screening, participatory consultations, dedicated feasibility teams for Component 4, strengthened GRM

safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: yes - GRM established at woreda and town levels including kebele level, with clear procedures and 45% women representation
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: Gender Action Plan prepared, 45% women participation target in WASHCOMs, gender-informed designs, MHM facilities, women's decision-making roles

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Project includes conflict mitigation tools through enhanced screening, community consultations, involvement of traditional leaders, documentation of processes, and strengthened grievance mechanisms
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: Ethiopia hosts 920,000+ refugees from neighboring countries, internally displaced populations due to sporadic unrest, recurring droughts and floods, political volatility with state of emergency lifted in May 2018
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: Political and social unrest affecting mobility and access, insecurity limiting contractor access to sites, potential for future unrest, labor influx and GBV risks during construction
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: yes - Ethiopia hosts over 920,000 refugees primarily from Somalia, Sudan, South Sudan, and Eritrea; internally displaced populations have doubled due to sporadic unrest; displacement adds pressure to localized water sources
